name: Telomerase reactivation reversed degenerative phenotypes in telomerase-deficient mice
summary: A severe telomere-dysfunction mouse model provides mechanistic support that restoring telomerase can reverse tissue pathology.
statement: In late-generation telomerase-deficient mice, inducible TERT reactivation extended telomeres, reduced DNA-damage signaling, and improved degenerative phenotypes across multiple tissues.
population_or_model: Late-generation telomerase-deficient mice with severe telomere dysfunction
time_horizon: Short-term reactivation experiment in adult mice
quantitative_note: The abstract reports reversal of organ degeneration in testes, spleen, intestine, and neural compartments, with recovery of olfactory avoidance behavior.
caveats: The model has engineered severe telomerase deficiency and does not represent normal human aging.
The intervention is genetic reactivation in mice, not a deployable human therapy.
Cancer-risk translation remains unresolved for human telomerase restoration.
